mechanism_narrative: Sustained, strong growth in nonfarm payrolls signals a robust economic backdrop, supporting corporate earnings and consumer spending. A positive 3-month moving average change in PAYEMS directly forecasts higher equity valuations.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS has shown consistent growth through 2017, indicating economic momentum.
failure_condition: The signal's rank information coefficient versus 20-day forward returns is not statistically greater than zero over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds rate rises above a key threshold, it signals a restrictive monetary policy that increases the discount rate for future earnings and tightens financial conditions, causing negative equity returns over the medium term.
evidence_refs: fact:The Federal Reserve began its tightening cycle in December 2015 and the rate is currently 1.16%.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS is above 1.0% and has risen in the prior 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous slowdown in industrial production growth and acceleration in consumer price inflation creates a stagflationary environment. This compresses corporate profit margins and erodes real returns, leading to negative equity performance.
candidate_trigger: 6-month change in INDPRO < 0 AND 6-month change in CPIAUCSL > 1.5%
failure_condition: The strategy of shorting the S&P 500 when both conditions are met fails to generate a positive Sharpe ratio over the full backtest period.

mechanism_narrative: In a low unemployment regime, strong retail sales growth indicates robust consumer confidence and spending. This translates directly to higher corporate revenues and drives positive equity market performance.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE is at a cyclical low of 4.1%, making consumer strength a key market driver.
failure_condition: The correlation between RSAFS 3-month momentum and S&P 500 20-day forward returns is not significantly positive during periods when UNRATE is below 5%.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts is a leading indicator of a broader economic slowdown, reflecting tighter credit and waning consumer confidence. This negatively impacts sectors beyond construction, leading to lower equity prices.
failure_condition: Periods following a >10% 3-month drop in housing starts do not show statistically significant negative S&P 500 returns compared to baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls to very low levels while inflation is accelerating, it signals an overheating economy. This forces the central bank to tighten policy more aggressively than expected, shocking equity markets negatively.
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE is 4.1%, fulfilling one leg of the condition.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 fails to produce negative returns on average in the 20 trading days following the simultaneous triggering of both conditions.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth is the most direct measure of economic health. Positive quarter-over-quarter growth in GDPC1 signals an expansionary environment supportive of corporate earnings and thus higher equity valuations.
evidence_refs: fact:Latest GDPC1 data from Q3 2017 shows continued economic expansion.
failure_condition: The sign of the quarter-over-quarter change in GDPC1 has a rank correlation below 0.05 with subsequent 60-day S&P 500 returns over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A surprisingly strong month-over-month increase in industrial production indicates that supply chains are robust and meeting demand, suggesting strong near-term corporate performance and boosting investor sentiment for short-term gains.
failure_condition: The hit rate of positive 5-day returns following a trigger event is less than 55% over the full backtest period.
